privacy.notes: Metadata-only Go binding contract. It describes request and response structs, transport fallback, diagnostics, provenance, errors, fixture gates, and module readiness without committing calculator logic or licensed payloads.

public_calculator_contract.notes: The Go binding request and response structs mirror the public calculator contract fields and only add transport metadata for service, CLI, and Arrow-file handoff.

transport_modes.notes: Fallback path for callers that need online request/response behavior.
Process-bound primary path for batch or local execution.
Inspectible file-exchange primary path for offline handoff using Arrow-compatible artifacts.

request_structs.name: GoBindingRequest

fields.name: schema_version
fields.notes: Binding schema version for the request envelope.

fields.name: calculator_id
fields.notes: Public calculator identifier from the shared contract.

fields.name: pricing_year
fields.notes: IHACPA pricing year or edition label.

fields.name: input_schema_version
fields.notes: Version of the declared input schema expected by the calculator.

fields.name: output_schema_version
fields.notes: Version of the declared output schema returned by the calculator.

fields.name: input_path
fields.notes: Local path for batch input when the binding uses a file boundary.

fields.name: output_path
fields.notes: Local path for batch output when the binding uses a file boundary.

fields.name: service_url
fields.notes: Configured service endpoint for the fallback transport path.

fields.name: arrow_input_path
fields.notes: Arrow-compatible input handoff path for the fallback path.

fields.name: arrow_output_path
fields.notes: Arrow-compatible output handoff path for the fallback path.

fields.name: correlation_id
fields.notes: Traceable caller-provided request identifier.

fields.name: metadata
fields.notes: Opaque caller metadata that must not carry calculator logic.

fields.name: fixture_gate
fields.notes: Declared fixture gate for synthetic-only or local-only payload handling.

request_structs.notes: Single versioned Go request envelope that mirrors the public calculator contract and adds only transport metadata.

response_structs.name: GoBindingResponse

fields.name: schema_version
fields.notes: Binding schema version for the response envelope.

fields.name: calculator_id
fields.notes: Echoes the public calculator identifier.

fields.name: pricing_year
fields.notes: Echoes the pricing year used during execution.

fields.name: mode
fields.notes: Declares the transport path that produced the result.

fields.name: success
fields.notes: True only when the request completed without a blocking error.

fields.name: status
fields.notes: Machine-readable execution state for the caller.

fields.name: message
fields.notes: Short human-readable summary of the result.

fields.name: warnings
fields.notes: Non-blocking warnings attached to the response.

fields.name: errors
fields.notes: Machine-readable error records emitted when the response does not succeed.

fields.name: diagnostics
fields.notes: Structured diagnostic record with pass, fail, and blocked checks.

fields.name: provenance
fields.notes: Provenance record for the request and any generated artifact.

fields.name: service_url
fields.notes: Service endpoint used to complete the request.

fields.name: output_path
fields.notes: Output path used by the fallback file boundary.

fields.name: arrow_output_path
fields.notes: Arrow-compatible output path used by the fallback file boundary.

fields.name: fixture_gate_state
fields.notes: Observed fixture-gate outcome for the request.

fields.name: module_readiness_state
fields.notes: Observed Go module readiness state for the binding bundle.

response_structs.notes: Single versioned Go response envelope that captures success, diagnostics, provenance, and transport-specific outputs.

diagnostics.notes: The Go request and response structs mirror the public calculator contract fields.
CLI and Arrow-file handoff are the primary transport boundary.
Service fallback is explicitly declared for online request/response callers.
Fixture gates remain synthetic-only or local-only and do not expose restricted payloads.
Module readiness is tracked as declarative metadata rather than implied implementation status.

provenance.notes: Name of the contract or validation command that produced the artifact.
Stable identifier for the Go binding contract bundle.
Path to the source manifest or fixture record used to derive the example.
Public source URL or local reference used for provenance.
Retrieval date in ISO-8601 date form.
Checksum algorithm used by the bundle.

errors.condition: request or response fields do not match the published Go binding schema
errors.notes: Use this when the envelope is structurally invalid.

errors.condition: the fallback service boundary cannot be reached
errors.notes: The caller may retry or switch to a fallback boundary.

errors.condition: CLI execution fails after a valid fallback request
errors.notes: The failure is transport-level, not calculator logic.

errors.condition: Arrow-file handoff cannot be read or written
errors.notes: Use for file-boundary errors in batch or offline handoff.

errors.condition: synthetic-only or local-only fixture gate is not satisfied
errors.notes: The request must stop before any restricted payload is handled.

errors.condition: the Go module is only scaffold-ready or blocked
errors.notes: Use this while the binding is still in contract-only or scaffolded form.

fixture_gates.notes: Committed examples must stay synthetic and metadata only.
Any licensed payloads stay local-only and user-supplied outside the repository.
The binding must remain pinned to the public calculator contract field set.

module_readiness.notes: The workstream is contract-ready but does not assert a published Go module.
The service boundary is the fallback transport path for the binding.
CLI and Arrow-file paths are declared as primary batch and offline handoff paths.

rules: Keep all examples synthetic and metadata only.
Mirror the public calculator contract fields explicitly.
Do not duplicate calculator math, thresholds, or adjustment logic in the Go binding.
Prefer CLI or Arrow-file first and use the service boundary when online request/response behavior is needed.
Keep diagnostics, provenance, and errors explicit and machine readable.
Keep fixture gates local-only and user-supplied for any licensed assets.
Treat module readiness as declarative metadata until an actual Go module is introduced.

examples.name: service-pass
examples.description: Synthetic service-bound fallback request and response example that passes the public calculator contract checks.

examples.name: cli-arrow-pass
examples.description: Synthetic CLI/Arrow-file primary example that passes batch handoff checks.

examples.name: diagnostics-pass
examples.description: Synthetic diagnostics example showing aligned contract, fixture, and readiness checks.

examples.name: binding-fail
examples.description: Synthetic failure example showing a blocked module readiness or transport boundary.

examples.name: diagnostics-fail
examples.description: Synthetic diagnostics example showing a blocked gate and explicit error codes.
